task.objective: Close the remaining executable authoritative-input wrapper paths in the Phase 1 synthetic kernel boundary by rejecting non-ordinary Array prototypes and Proxy-backed authoritative values before caller-controlled behavior can influence semantic validation or cloning.

requirements: Materialize governance/tasks/KERNEL-VS-CONV-002.json as the first task artifact.
Preserve all KERNEL-VS-001 product semantics.
Preserve the completed KVS-BND-001, KVS-RSN-001, and KVS-SCOPE-001 remediations.
Resolve KVS-BND-002.
Authoritative Array values must not dispatch through caller-controlled Array prototypes during boundary validation or authoritative cloning.
Reject authoritative Arrays whose direct prototype is not the ordinary Array.prototype expected for a plain JSON-compatible Array.
A null-prototype Array must be rejected at the public kernel boundary with KernelBoundaryError.
An Array subclass instance must be rejected at the public kernel boundary with KernelBoundaryError.
An Array whose prototype was replaced with a custom object must be rejected at the public kernel boundary with KernelBoundaryError.
Prefer the existing INVALID_ARRAY issue category unless a narrower KernelBoundaryError category is materially clearer.
The error path for a non-ordinary top-level observations Array should be: $.observations.
The Array prototype check must happen before caller-controlled Array prototype methods can execute.

Do not call caller-owned forEach, map, filter, find, sort, flatMap, Array iterator, or spread iteration on an authoritative caller-owned Array before it has been structurally cloned into a fresh ordinary kernel-owned Array.
Replace authoritative cloning through value.map(...) with bounded indexed cloning into a fresh ordinary Array.
The safe Array clone must use the previously validated dense length and own data indices.
The safe clone must not invoke a caller-owned Array instance method.
Semantic shape validation must not depend on caller-controlled Array prototype dispatch.

Use the smaller coherent design between safely cloning the structurally validated authoritative graph before semantic shape validation and replacing every pre-clone caller-owned Array instance-method dispatch with bounded indexed logic.
Do not leave a mixed boundary where some pre-clone validation uses safe index iteration but other semantic validation still dispatches through caller-owned Array methods.
Add focused tests proving observations Array with Object.setPrototypeOf(array, null) is rejected with KernelBoundaryError and not ordinary TypeError.
Add focused tests proving rules Array with Object.setPrototypeOf(array, null) is rejected.
Add focused tests proving an Array subclass instance is rejected.
Add focused tests proving an Array subclass overriding forEach does not execute the overridden forEach body.
Add focused tests proving an Array subclass overriding map does not execute the overridden map body.
Add focused tests proving a custom Array prototype attempting to skip validation does not execute.
Add focused tests proving a custom Array prototype attempting to substitute clone output does not execute.
Add focused tests proving nested Evidence Contract requirementIds with a non-ordinary Array prototype is rejected.
Add focused tests proving nested Observation limitations with a non-ordinary Array prototype is rejected.
Add focused tests proving ordinary dense Arrays remain accepted.
Record execution counters for overridden forEach and map tests with expected counts of 0.
Resolve KVS-BND-003.
Proxy-backed authoritative values must not participate in the authoritative input graph.
Reject Proxy-backed authoritative values before operations capable of invoking caller-controlled Proxy traps.

Proxy rejection must occur before instanceof Date, instanceof Map, instanceof Set, Array.isArray, Object.getPrototypeOf, Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors, Reflect.ownKeys on caller-derived descriptor behavior, and caller property reads.
Use a built-in capability available in the pinned Node 24 runtime.
Do not add an external dependency.
Proxy rejection applies recursively to the complete authoritative input graph.

Proxy rejection includes at minimum Proxy-wrapped root input, evaluation, Claim, Evidence Contract, Evidence Contract selection provenance, Evidence Requirement, requiredObserver, Observation, observer, Rule, Rule predicate, Rule effect, Rule authority, and authoritative Arrays.
Prefer a deterministic KernelBoundaryError issue category PROXY_INPUT unless an existing category has exactly correct semantics.
The relative input path must identify the Proxy-backed value.
Add focused tests for Proxy-wrapped root input.
Add focused tests for Proxy-wrapped evaluation.
Add focused tests for Proxy-wrapped Observation.
Add focused tests for Proxy-wrapped observations Array.
Add focused tests for Proxy-wrapped nested Observation limitations Array.
Add focused tests for Proxy-wrapped Rule effect.
Add a focused test for revoked Proxy input if the selected built-in proxy detector can identify it without invoking traps.
Proxy regression tests must instrument get, getPrototypeOf, ownKeys, and getOwnPropertyDescriptor trap execution counters where applicable.
For a Proxy rejected by the early boundary guard, expected trap execution count is zero.
A Proxy must not be able to lie about descriptors and then supply a different property value during authoritative cloning.
A Proxy must not be able to convert a malformed authoritative record into a different validated clone.
Proxy rejection is a kernel boundary shape rule. It is not a Proofrail Verdict. It is not Evidence. It must not create a product reason code.
Preserve KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING as the single internal missing-Evidence condition reason-code literal.
Preserve exact Rule rejection of KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING.
Preserve HARN_ Rule reason-code rejection.
Preserve Observation declared-evaluation-scope validation.
Preserve all previously implemented deterministic behavior.

Do not weaken Claim is not Evidence, exact Observation satisfaction matching, primitive type distinction, limitation handling, Evidence Contract selection provenance restrictions, Rule authority provenance restrictions, duplicate identity rejection, invalid reference rejection, Claim/Contract scope coverage, Contract/Requirement bidirectional membership, Observation evaluation-scope consistency, Verdict precedence, lower-precedence reason retention, deterministic normalization, canonical serialization, deterministic SHA-256-derived identities, Evidence Lineage coverage, caller-input isolation, deep bundle immutability, forbidden model/inference authority fields, Array descriptor validation, sparse Array rejection, accessor-backed Array index rejection before getter execution, exact reserved kernel missing-Evidence reason-code separation, HARN_ namespace separation, public kernel surface restrictions, package dependency direction.

requiredArtifacts: caller-owned Array method-dispatch elimination before safe clone
second kernel vertical-slice convergence Builder review

requiredInvariants: Non-ordinary Array prototypes cannot enter semantic authoritative validation.
Caller-controlled Array prototype methods cannot execute during boundary validation or authoritative cloning.
Null-prototype Arrays cannot escape later as ordinary TypeError.
Authoritative Array cloning produces a fresh ordinary kernel-owned Array.
Proxy-backed authoritative values are rejected before Proxy traps execute.
Proxy-backed values cannot lie about authoritative descriptors or values.
Every prototype or Proxy regression fails through KernelBoundaryError.
Previous Array accessor getter execution count remains zero.
Previous sparse Array rejection remains deterministic.
KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING remains the missing Evidence Requirement condition reason code.
HARN_ Rule reason codes remain rejected.
Observations outside declared evaluation scope remain rejected.
Valid-scope unmatched Observations remain accepted boundary input.
Identical normalized valid inputs continue producing deeply equal bundle output and stable bundle identity.
No production package outside contracts and kernel exists.
No repository inspection, target-code execution, verification execution, Policy runtime, adapter, delivery, network, or inference path is introduced.

stopConditions: a general product reason-code registry is required
a new production package is required
PR #5 or exact reviewed head identity differs from preflight
